featureName: BUG-026-003: handleDomainExtracted has zero unit coverage despite five same-named tests

completedPhaseClaims.summary: Created bug packet for handleDomainExtracted unit-coverage gap surfaced by stochastic-quality-sweep round 10 of 20 (regression trigger, seed 20520512). Discovery showed 0.0% coverage despite five same-named tests that only exercise ValidateDomainExtractResponse on struct literals.

Refactored internal/pipeline/domain_subscriber.go to introduce DomainDB interface and changed DB field type from *pgxpool.Pool to DomainDB. Constructor signature unchanged so cmd/core/services.go:152 continues to compile and behave identically. Added pgconn import. Added mockDomainDB helper plus 6 TestHandleDomainExtractedInvocation_* tests to internal/pipeline/domain_subscriber_test.go covering success UPDATE+Ack, failure-status UPDATE+Ack with domain_extracted_at = NOW() per S-001, invalid-JSON Ack-without-DB, missing-artifact-id Ack-without-DB, success-path DB-error → Nak below domainMaxDeliver, and failure-path DB-error → Nak. git diff --stat cmd/core/services.go exited 0 with no output proving the production call site is byte-identical.

All six new TestHandleDomainExtractedInvocation_* tests pass. Whole-package re-run (go test -count=1 ./internal/pipeline/...) exits 0 with no pre-existing test regressions. Coverage on handleDomainExtracted moved from 0.0% to 96.8% (delta +96.8 points). handleDomainDeliveryFailure moved from 55.6% to 81.5% as a side effect of new DB-error tests. Coverage exceeds the >= 80% acceptance criterion.

go build ./..., go vet ./internal/pipeline/... ./cmd/core/..., and gofmt -l on both edited files all exit 0. No new dependencies introduced. Production refactor uses one new pgconn import already present in the existing dependency tree (transitively pulled in by pgx). No deprecation, no API surface widening beyond the new DomainDB interface required for testability.

Spec 026 report.md finding TG1 row 171 amended in place to reflect truth-in-evidence: the original 'Fixed — added domain_subscriber_test.go' claim is annotated as struct-validation-only with a forward link to BUG-026-003. Original text preserved in the amendment so future readers see exactly what was claimed and what was actually delivered. No other content in spec 026 modified.

Regression-trigger probe (parent stochastic-quality-sweep round 10 of 20, seed 20520512) discovered the test-fabrication finding underlying this bug: handleDomainExtracted at 0.0% unit coverage despite five same-named tests in domain_subscriber_test.go that only exercise ValidateDomainExtractResponse on struct literals. Cross-spec coherence with 035 (read-time transform, no schema change) and 037 (preserves Go-owned data ownership) probed and confirmed intact.

Simplification probe — refactor was already minimal (one new interface, one field type change, one new import). No further simplification opportunity identified. mockDomainDB helper is the smallest possible mock (records sql + args, returns configurable error). The six new tests share no duplicated setup that would warrant table-driven consolidation because each asserts on a structurally different SQL/Ack/Nak combination.

Stabilization probe — the new tests stabilize the regression-protection surface for handleDomainExtracted. Coverage gain (0.0% -> 96.8%) means a future refactor that breaks the SQL UPDATE shape, swaps Ack/Nak, or drops domain_extracted_at = NOW() now fails ./smackerel.sh test unit instead of only failing the live-stack E2E. mockDomainDB uses sync.Mutex to be safe under -race.

Security probe — change surface is purely test-level (one new exported interface DomainDB and a private mockDomainDB test helper). No new attack surface, no new auth/authz path, no new external input handling. The DomainDB interface only exposes Exec(ctx, sql, args...) — narrower than the full pgxpool.Pool surface — which is a minor security-posture improvement (least privilege).

Validation gate satisfied: (a) all six new tests pass, (b) coverage on handleDomainExtracted is 96.8% exceeding the >= 80% criterion, (c) no pre-existing test regressed, (d) production call site byte-identical (git diff --stat exits 0 with no output for cmd/core/services.go), (e) build/vet/format all exit 0, (f) live-stack defense-in-depth tests/e2e/domain_e2e_test.go::TestE2E_DomainExtraction is unmodified. bug.md status banner updated to Fixed/Verified/Closed at packetization time because the closure happened in the same execution round.

scopeProgress.name: Refactor `DB` to `DomainDB` interface and add real handler-invocation tests

executionHistory.summary: Single-round closure within stochastic-quality-sweep round 10 of 20 parent-expanded regression-to-doc mode. All 6 phases (bug → implement → test → audit → docs → validate) executed and recorded. Production refactor backward-compatible. Coverage on handleDomainExtracted: 0.0% → 96.8%. Spec 026 report.md finding TG1 amended in place. cmd/core/services.go untouched. Live-stack E2E preserved.

failures.summary: Coverage probe revealed handleDomainExtracted at 0.0% despite five TestHandleDomainExtracted_* tests in domain_subscriber_test.go. Source inspection confirmed those tests only exercise ValidateDomainExtractResponse on struct literals, never the receiver method. Closed in the same round.
